item.title: Working With Class Based Components
item.description: Note: I built a little app to demonstrate how to work with class-based components. You can view the repository here: Github Repo React has evolved through the years, and as a result functional components are much more powerful than they used to be.

item.title: React Props: A Primer
item.description: In React, components make it really easy to duplicate parts of a website with slight variations. For example, say you wanted to have a comment section underneath your blog posts. Each comment would look very similar, with an avatar, a date, a text block, and maybe even some like and dislike buttons.

item.title: JSX: A Few Notes
item.description: JSX is getting translated into JavaScript, but it isn’t a one-to-one translation. There are a few syntactical notes to be aware of. Inline Styling In HTML, inline styling is done using the “style” attribute and giving it a string of CSS.

item.title: How to Generate a React App
item.description: This is really simple. Make sure that you have node installed, and that it’s a modern enough version of node to include npx. Then, simply type the command npx create-react-app my-new-app into the terminal.
